name: pre-commit (Git Hooks Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); language-agnostic git hooks manager

performance.notes: Hook execution time varies; cached environments make repeated runs fast

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-language git hooks framework. Configure in .pre-commit-config.yaml. 1000+ hooks available. Hooks from remote repositories. Isolated environments per hook. Languages: Python, Node, Go, Rust, Ruby, Swift, and more. Popular hooks: black, ruff, mypy, prettier, eslint, yamllint, detect-secrets, gitleaks. CI integration (pre-commit.ci). Essential for enforcing code quality standards across teams. Language-agnostic unlike husky (Node-only).

gotchas: Hook environment setup adds time on first run
Some hooks modify files (can surprise developers)
Hook configuration requires maintenance as tools evolve
best_when: Enforcing code quality in multi-language or Python projects
Language-agnostic git hooks (vs husky for Node-only)
CI code quality enforcement via pre-commit.ci
Any team wanting consistent code formatting and linting
avoid_when: Node-only projects with existing husky setup
Repositories where hooks would annoy contributors (use CI instead)
summary: pre-commit is a language-agnostic git hooks framework. Configure hooks in .pre-commit-config.yaml from 1000+ available hooks. Isolated environments per hook. Supports Python, Node, Go, Rust, and more. Popular for running black/ruff/mypy/prettier before commits. pre-commit.ci for cloud-based enforcement. Essential for teams wanting consistent code quality standards across any language stack.
